headline: El Salvador Eliminates Trachoma, Becoming Central America's First
meta_description: El Salvador has become the first country in Central America to eliminate trachoma, the world's leading infectious cause of blindness.
summary_short: El Salvador achieves a historic health milestone by eliminating trachoma, the world's leading infectious cause of blindness, after finding no active cases.
body:
El Salvador has reached a remarkable health milestone: it is now the first country in Central America to eliminate trachoma, a bacterial eye infection that remains the world's leading infectious cause of blindness.

The achievement comes after nationwide surveillance found no active cases of the disease across the country. This breakthrough represents years of public health effort and marks a turning point for a region where trachoma has long threatened vision and quality of life.

Trachoma, caused by the bacterium *Chlamydia trachomatis*, thrives in areas with poor sanitation and limited access to clean water. For decades, it has silently stolen sight from millions worldwide, particularly in low-income communities. El Salvador's success demonstrates that elimination is possible—and offers hope to neighboring countries still battling the disease.
